items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Hundreds of Salesforce customers in new 'Salesforce Aura' campaign targeting overly permissive cloud configurations
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) - Mass scanning of Salesforce Experience Cloud using modified AuraInspector tool

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: AllSecure CEO targeted via fake LinkedIn interview; developers targeted via Gogs vulnerability for LFS object manipulation
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain)

items.name: APT28 (Fancy Bear, Russia-linked)
items.targets: Ukrainian forces in long-term espionage operations; Signal and WhatsApp accounts of government officials globally
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1056 (Input Capture), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - Revived advanced malware toolkit for cyber-espionage

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT, MOIS)
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure and corporate networks using new Dindoor backdoor malware
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer)

items.name: Iranian threat actors (multiple groups)
items.targets: Medtech firm Stryker (wiper attack claimed), U.S. critical infrastructure, Israel; psychological warfare via fake 'Shelter Danger' calls
items.ttps: T1485 (Data Destruction), T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1566 (Phishing), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information) - GPS spoofing attacks near Iran

items.name: Chinese Nexus Actors
items.targets: Qatar with PlugX lures tied to Middle East conflict and Iranian tensions
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer) - PlugX remote access trojan deployment

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: U.S. electric cooperative, RWB Consulting Engineers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: netCOMPONENTS and multiple victims in ongoing campaigns
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: BeatBanker (Android malware)
items.targets: Android users via dual-mode trojan with phishing capabilities
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1417 (Input Capture on Mobile), T1582 (Clipboard Data on Mobile)

items.name: SurxRAT (Android malware)
items.targets: Android users using LLMs for enhanced phishing and data theft
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1417 (Input Capture on Mobile), T1409 (Stored Application Data)

items.name: GhostClaw/GhostLoader
items.targets: Developers via fake OpenClaw npm package masquerading as legitimate development tools
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File)

items.name: A0Backdoor operators
items.targets: Organizations via Teams social engineering campaign and phishing operations
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1055 (Process Injection)

items.name: VIP Keylogger MaaS operators
items.targets: Mass credential theft campaign using steganography techniques

items.name: Tycoon 2FA PhaaS platform (disrupted)
items.targets: Platform providing phishing-as-a-service capabilities, disrupted by law enforcement
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1621 (Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation)

canada_landscape.summary: In the last 48 hours, Canadian entities were referenced in multiple contexts: Meta disabled 150K accounts linked to Southeast Asia scam centers in a global crackdown affecting Canada; Iranian cyber operations and phishing campaigns had Canadian targets mentioned; and alleged data listings involving Canada appeared on dark web forums. No direct attacks on major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported.
canada_landscape.retailers: None identified. No incidents reported affecting Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's, or other major Canadian retail chains.
canada_landscape.financial: None identified. No specific incidents reported affecting CIBC, RBC, TD Bank, Scotiabank, BMO, or other Canadian financial institutions.
